descriptions.description: The NASAMS (National Advanced Surface-to-Air Missile System) is a mobile, ground-based air defense system developed jointly by Kongsberg Defence & Aerospace (Norway) and U.S. partners. The system integrates air-to-air missiles (AIM-120 AMRAAM, AIM-9X Sidewinder) adapted for ground launch in the surface-to-air role. NASAMS entered service in the Norwegian Air Force in 1997 and has since been adopted by numerous NATO and allied nations. The system combines the AN/MPQ-64 Sentinel radar with mobile launcher vehicles, providing rapid-response capability for air defense across a wide altitude envelope.
descriptions.shortDescription: Norwegian-American mobile ground-based air defense system firing adapted air-to-air missiles.

descriptions.description: The NASAMS system consists of a command post, the AN/MPQ-64 Sentinel phased-array radar, and transporter-launcher vehicles equipped with six-round missile canisters. The platform fires multiple variants of the AIM-120 AMRAAM missile, including the extended-range AMRAAM-ER, and integrates the AIM-9X Sidewinder for close-range engagement. A typical battery comprises up to four firing units with three launchers each, one radar unit, a command center, and an electro-optical sensor vehicle. The modular architecture allows for rapid deployment on truck platforms and integration with NATO air defense networks.
descriptions.shortDescription: Mobile system with AN/MPQ-64 radar and launcher vehicles firing AMRAAM and Sidewinder missiles.
